title: 2018. évi LIV. törvény az üzleti titok védelméről
title_en: Act LIV of 2018 on the Protection of Trade Secrets
description: Hungary's trade secrets protection legislation implementing Directive (EU) 2016/943 on the protection of undisclosed know-how and business information (trade secrets) against their unlawful acquisition, use and disclosure.

provisions.title: § 1 — Purpose and Scope
provisions.content: The purpose of this Act is to lay down rules on the protection of trade secrets against their unlawful acquisition, use and disclosure. This Act implements Directive (EU) 2016/943 of the European Parliament and of the Council on the protection of undisclosed know-how and business information (trade secrets) against their unlawful acquisition, use and disclosure. This Act shall apply to the unlawful acquisition, use and disclosure of trade secrets.

provisions.title: § 2 — Definition of Trade Secret
provisions.content: For the purposes of this Act, 'trade secret' means information which: (a) is secret in the sense that it is not generally known among or readily accessible to persons within the circles that normally deal with the kind of information in question; (b) has commercial value because it is secret; (c) has been subject to reasonable steps under the circumstances, by the person lawfully in control of the information, to keep it secret. This definition corresponds to Article 2(1) of Directive (EU) 2016/943.

provisions.title: § 3 — Lawful Acquisition
provisions.content: The acquisition of a trade secret shall be considered lawful where obtained by: (a) independent discovery or creation; (b) observation, study, disassembly or testing of a product or object that has been made available to the public or that is lawfully in the possession of the acquirer; (c) the exercise of the right of workers or workers' representatives to information and consultation in accordance with Hungarian law and EU law; (d) any other practice which, under the circumstances, is in conformity with honest commercial practices.

provisions.title: § 4 — Unlawful Acquisition
provisions.content: The acquisition of a trade secret without the consent of the trade secret holder shall be considered unlawful where carried out by: (a) unauthorised access to, appropriation of, or copying of any documents, objects, materials, substances or electronic files lawfully under the control of the trade secret holder, which contain the trade secret or from which the trade secret can be deduced; (b) any other conduct which, under the circumstances, is considered contrary to honest commercial practices. A person who knowingly acquires a trade secret that has been previously unlawfully acquired by a third person shall also be deemed to have unlawfully acquired that trade secret.

provisions.title: § 5 — Unlawful Use and Disclosure
provisions.content: The use or disclosure of a trade secret shall be considered unlawful where carried out without the consent of the trade secret holder by a person who: (a) has acquired the trade secret unlawfully; (b) is in breach of a confidentiality agreement or any other duty not to disclose the trade secret; (c) is in breach of a contractual or any other duty to limit the use of the trade secret. The production, offering or placing on the market of infringing goods, or the importation, export or storage of infringing goods for those purposes, shall be considered unlawful use where the person carrying out such activities knew or ought to have known that the trade secret was used unlawfully.

provisions.title: § 6 — Exceptions — Whistleblowing
provisions.content: The acquisition, use or disclosure of a trade secret shall be considered lawful where: (a) it is necessary for revealing misconduct, wrongdoing or illegal activity, provided that the respondent acted for the purpose of protecting the general public interest; (b) it is disclosed by workers to their representatives as part of the legitimate exercise of their representative function; (c) it is necessary for the protection of a legitimate interest recognised by Hungarian law or EU law. This provision ensures alignment with whistleblower protection under Directive (EU) 2019/1937.

provisions.title: § 7 — Exceptions — Journalistic Freedom
provisions.content: The acquisition, use or disclosure of a trade secret shall not be considered unlawful where it is carried out for the exercise of the right to freedom of expression and information as set out in the Hungarian Fundamental Law and the Charter of Fundamental Rights of the European Union, including respect for the freedom and pluralism of the media.

provisions.title: § 8 — Civil Remedies
provisions.content: The trade secret holder may apply to the court for the following measures against the infringer: (a) cessation of or prohibition on the unlawful acquisition, use or disclosure of the trade secret; (b) prohibition on the production, offering, placing on the market or use of infringing goods, or the importation, export or storage of infringing goods; (c) the destruction or delivery up of any documents, objects, materials or electronic files containing the trade secret; (d) the recall of infringing goods from the market. The court may also order the publication of the judgment.

provisions.title: § 9 — Damages
provisions.content: Any person who unlawfully acquires, uses or discloses a trade secret shall be liable for damages in accordance with the general rules on damages. In assessing damages, the court shall consider: (a) the negative economic consequences, including lost profits, suffered by the trade secret holder; (b) any unfair profits made by the infringer; (c) in appropriate cases, the amount of royalties or fees that would have been due if the infringer had requested authorisation to use the trade secret. The court may, as an alternative, set the damages on a lump-sum basis.

provisions.title: § 10 — Injunctive Relief
provisions.content: The court may grant provisional and precautionary measures at the request of the trade secret holder, including: (a) the cessation of or prohibition against the use or disclosure of the trade secret on a provisional basis; (b) the seizure or delivery up of suspected infringing goods; (c) an order requiring the respondent to provide a security or guarantee. The applicant shall provide evidence that the trade secret exists, that the applicant is the trade secret holder, and that the trade secret has been or is about to be unlawfully acquired, used or disclosed.

provisions.title: § 11 — Preservation of Confidentiality in Proceedings
provisions.content: In proceedings concerning the unlawful acquisition, use or disclosure of a trade secret, the court shall take specific measures to preserve the confidentiality of the trade secret during and after the proceedings. Such measures may include: (a) restricting access to documents or hearings to a limited number of persons; (b) restricting access to the evidence or hearings to a limited number of persons; (c) making available non-confidential versions of judicial decisions, with the parts containing trade secrets removed. This provision implements Article 9 of Directive (EU) 2016/943.

provisions.title: § 12 — Limitation Period
provisions.content: Actions for the protection of trade secrets shall be subject to a limitation period of three years. The limitation period shall begin on the date on which the trade secret holder became aware or had reason to become aware of the unlawful acquisition, use or disclosure. The limitation period shall not exceed six years from the date of the unlawful act, regardless of when the trade secret holder became aware of it.

provisions.title: § 13 — Abusive Litigation
provisions.content: Where a claim for the protection of a trade secret is manifestly unfounded and the applicant has initiated the legal proceedings with the intention of unjustly delaying or restricting the respondent's access to the market, harassing the respondent, or intimidating the respondent, the court may: (a) award damages to the respondent; (b) order the applicant to bear the costs of the proceedings, including reasonable attorney's fees. The court shall take into account the respondent's interests in its assessment.

provisions.title: § 14 — Relationship with Employment Law
provisions.content: The provisions of this Act shall apply to the acquisition, use and disclosure of trade secrets in the context of employment relationships, without prejudice to the rights and obligations established by the Labour Code. Employees shall be bound by a duty of confidentiality with respect to trade secrets that they become aware of in the course of their employment. The duty of confidentiality shall survive the termination of the employment relationship unless otherwise agreed.

provisions.title: § 15 — Trade Secrets and Public Data
provisions.content: Information claimed as a trade secret by a body performing public duties or a state-owned enterprise may not be withheld from the public if the disclosure of such information is in the overriding public interest. The test for overriding public interest shall take into account the use of public funds, the potential for corruption, and the significance of the information for public accountability. This provision is subject to the provisions of Act CXII of 2011 on Informational Self-Determination and Freedom of Information.

provisions.title: § 16 — Cyber Theft of Trade Secrets
provisions.content: The unlawful acquisition of trade secrets by means of unauthorised access to electronic information systems, interception of electronic communications, or exploitation of vulnerabilities in information systems constitutes unlawful acquisition under Section 4 and shall also be subject to the criminal provisions of the Criminal Code (2012. évi C. törvény, §§ 422-424) on offences against information systems.

provisions.title: § 17 — Technical Protection Measures
provisions.content: For the purposes of Section 2(c), reasonable steps to keep information secret shall include, but not be limited to: (a) classification of information by sensitivity level; (b) access controls and authentication measures for electronic information systems; (c) encryption of electronic communications and stored data; (d) non-disclosure agreements with employees, contractors and business partners; (e) physical access controls; (f) regular audits of compliance with security measures.

provisions.title: § 18 — Cross-Border Enforcement
provisions.content: Where the unlawful acquisition, use or disclosure of a trade secret involves parties in more than one EU Member State, the court shall apply the rules of Regulation (EU) No 1215/2012 (Brussels I Recast) on jurisdiction and the recognition and enforcement of judgments. The court shall give effect to provisional measures ordered by courts of other Member States in accordance with that Regulation.

provisions.title: § 19 — Relationship with Other Laws
provisions.content: This Act shall apply without prejudice to: (a) the rules of competition law; (b) the rules on intellectual property, including patent, copyright and design rights; (c) the rules on employment law; (d) the rules on data protection, including Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (GDPR); (e) the rules on freedom of information. In the event of a conflict between this Act and a sector-specific law, the sector-specific law shall prevail in its field of application.

provisions.title: § 20 — Entry into Force and Transitional Provisions
provisions.content: This Act shall enter into force on 8 August 2018. This Act implements Directive (EU) 2016/943 of the European Parliament and of the Council on the protection of undisclosed know-how and business information (trade secrets) against their unlawful acquisition, use and disclosure. Claims arising from acts committed before the entry into force of this Act shall be governed by the provisions previously in effect.

definitions.definition: Information that is secret, has commercial value because it is secret, and has been subject to reasonable steps to keep it secret, as defined in Article 2(1) of Directive (EU) 2016/943.
Goods the design, characteristics, functioning, production process or marketing of which significantly benefits from trade secrets unlawfully acquired, used or disclosed.
Any natural or legal person lawfully controlling a trade secret.
